text: Death Valley National Park
zh: 死亡谷国家公园

text: Elsewhere in the Mojave, just north of Joshua
zh: 莫哈韦沙漠的另一处，就在约书亚

text: Tree, A seemingly endless noon grills a vast expanse of barren dunes.
zh: 树以北，看似无尽的正午骄阳炙烤着一片广袤荒芜的沙丘。

text: Lying far below sea level, this is Death Valley, the most exotic of our national parks.
zh: 低于海平面之下，这里就是死亡谷，我们国家公园中最奇特的一个。

text: East of the Sierra Nevada, between the arid Great Basin and the Mojave,
zh: 位于内华达山脉以东，干旱的大盆地与莫哈韦沙漠之间，

text: this is the largest park in the contiguous United States.
zh: 这里是美国本土面积最大的国家公园。

text: Though there's often snow on the distant Paramount Mountains, it can be the hottest place in North America.
zh: 尽管远处帕纳明特山脉常年积雪，这里却可能是北美最热的地方。

text: In August, a brutal 120 degrees Fahrenheit in the shade,
zh: 八月里，阴凉处也高达华氏120度，

text: if shade can be found. Hot enough to melt a pair of shoes.
zh: 如果还能找到阴凉的话。热到足以把一双鞋熔化。

text: A sign at Badwater Basin marks the lowest spot on the continent.
zh: 恶水盆地的一块标牌标示着北美大陆的最低点。

text: A drop from Death Valley's highest mountain, 11 ,049 -foot telescope peak to Badwater Basin,
zh: 从死亡谷最高的山峰——海拔11049英尺的望远镜峰，到恶水盆地的落差，

text: is twice the depth of Grand Canyon.
zh: 是大峡谷深度的两倍。

text: There were large lakes here once, in the prehistoric past.
zh: 在史前时代，这里曾有过大片湖泊。

text: But the Earth's climates changed, and the lakes dried up.
zh: 但地球气候变迁，湖泊逐渐干涸。

text: Yet, Death Valley exudes a strange beauty all its own.
zh: 然而，死亡谷散发出一种独属于它自己的奇异之美。

text: As America's poet Mark Strand has discovered, its million -plus areas of
zh: 正如美国诗人马克·斯特兰德所发现的那样，这里数百万英亩的

text: yellows, oranges, The orange, faint reds and purple smudges
zh: 黄色、橙色，

text: of manganese suggest a Martian landscape, or what we believe the reaches of Mars to be like.
zh: 锰矿留下的淡红与紫色斑斓色调，让人联想到火星地貌，或是我们想象中火星的样子。

text: Precious water from rare desert springs sustains wildlife.
zh: 珍贵的沙漠泉水滋养着野生动物。

text: Humans were drawn here in the 1900s by the land's mineral wealth. Mines were constructed.
zh: 20世纪初，人类被这里丰富的矿产吸引而来，矿场随之建立。

text: Borax, a mineral with many industrial uses, was extracted from the ancient salt crusts lining the lakes that had once shimmered here.
zh: 硼砂，一种用途广泛的工业矿物，从这里曾波光粼粼的湖泊留下的古老盐壳中被开采出来。

text: The borax was hauled to the railroad in huge 20 -mule team wagons.
zh: 硼砂由庞大的二十骡队马车运往铁路。

text: Tent City sprang up which gave way to settlements of wood and stone
zh: 帐篷城随之兴起，后来又被木石建筑的定居点取代，

text: Rhyolite is one of them
zh: 莱奥莱特就是其中之一

text: Today, Rhyolite, just outside the park is a ghost town and well worth a visit
zh: 如今，就在公园外的莱奥莱特是一座鬼镇，非常值得一游

text: Now, these ghost towns bleach in the torrid sun
zh: 如今，这些鬼镇在炙热的阳光下渐渐褪色

text: Until one day, they simply fade back
zh: 直到有一天，它们终将

text: into the desert all isn't ghostly
zh: 重新归于沙漠。但并非一切都显得阴森

text: After a lengthy stretch of bareness, of raw, harsh wasteland a visitor encounters this.
zh: 经过一段漫长空旷、原始荒凉的旷野之后，游客会遇见这样一处景象。

text: No, it isn't a mirage or a sign of sunstroke.
zh: 不，这不是海市蜃楼，也不是中暑的幻觉。

text: This architectural hybrid, part Spanish, part Mediterranean, part Hollywood back lot
zh: 这座建筑融合了西班牙风格、地中海风情，还带着好莱坞片场的味道，

text: is Scotty's castle the most famous man -made attraction in Death Valley
zh: 这就是斯科蒂城堡，死亡谷中最著名的人造景点，

text: though he never really owned it it's named after Death Valley Scotty
zh: 尽管他其实从未真正拥有过它，它是以“死亡谷斯科蒂”命名的，

text: the wildest two -legged critter ever to dwell in these parts
zh: 他是曾在这片土地上生活过的最狂野的两足生物，

text: as a young cowboy Scotty travels with Buffalo Bill's Wild West show.
zh: 年轻时，斯科蒂曾是牛仔，随“水牛比尔”西部秀四处巡演。

text: Performing as a trick rider and sharpshooter. But these aren't Scotty's only talents.
zh: 他表演特技骑术和神枪手绝活。但这些并非斯科蒂唯一的本事。

text: He has an inborn knack for, you might say, playing tricks with the truth.
zh: 他天生擅长——可以说——玩弄真相。

text: Plain folks call it lying. One of his imaginary grub stakes is
zh: 普通人称之为撒谎。他虚构的众多“财路”之一，便是

text: a pie -in -the -sky gold mine in a little -known valley.
zh: 一个鲜为人知的山谷里，一座虚无缥缈的金矿。

text: Well, Scotty goes east to Chicago to sell shares in his pipe dream mine to unsuspecting businessmen.
zh: 于是，斯科蒂东行来到芝加哥，向毫不知情的商人们兜售他那虚构金矿的股份。

text: As luck would have it, he peddles a third of the shares to Albert Johnson, the wealthy president of the National Life Insurance Company.
zh: 机缘巧合下，他把三分之一的股份卖给了阿尔伯特·约翰逊——国民人寿保险公司的富有总裁。

text: Scotty had corralled a real sucker. Poor Johnson hadn't seen so much as a single gold nugget.
zh: 斯科蒂算是钓到了一条大鱼。可怜的约翰逊连一块金矿石都没见过。

text: Pleased with himself, Scotty returns to Death Valley, leaving the swindled Albert Johnson behind in Chicago.
zh: 斯科蒂洋洋得意地回到死亡谷，把被骗的阿尔伯特·约翰逊留在了芝加哥。

text: Or does he? A telegram reaches Scotty.
zh: 然而事情并未就此结束。一封电报传到了斯科蒂手中。

text: Albert Johnson is coming to Death Valley to see for himself this gold mine of which he is now part owner.
zh: 阿尔伯特·约翰逊要亲自前往死亡谷，看看这座他如今已是股东之一的金矿。

text: Scotty's alarmed, but can't this weigh Johnson from coming out?
zh: 斯科蒂大为惊慌，但又怎能阻止约翰逊前来呢？

text: En route to Death Valley, Albert Johnson and Scotty are halted by masked bandits.
zh: 在前往死亡谷的路上，阿尔伯特·约翰逊与斯科蒂被蒙面强盗拦下。

text: It's a holdup. Rumor has it that Scotty has a holdup staged as a delaying tactic.
zh: 这是一场“抢劫”。有传言说，这场抢劫其实是斯科蒂精心策划的拖延之计。

text: In any case, Johnson returns to Chicago, never having seen the bogus goldmine.
zh: 无论如何，约翰逊最终返回芝加哥，始终没能见到那座子虚乌有的金矿。

text: But Johnson makes another trip. Scotty's beside himself.
zh: 但约翰逊又一次踏上旅程。斯科蒂心急如焚。

text: It seems that Johnson has some serious health problems and finds the desert air healing.
zh: 原来，约翰逊患有一些严重的健康问题，而沙漠的空气似乎有疗愈之效。

text: As a matter of fact, he hasn't felt so well in years as he does in Death Valley.
zh: 事实上，他多年来从未像在死亡谷这般感觉良好。

text: His visits become longer and more frequent and soon he's bringing his wife Bessie along with him.
zh: 他来访的次数越来越频繁，时间也越来越长，不久后还带上了妻子贝西。

text: Bessie too likes the desert, but she dislikes sleeping in a tent among rattlesnakes and scorpions.
zh: 贝西也喜欢这片沙漠，但她不喜欢在满是响尾蛇和蝎子的帐篷里过夜。

text: Bessie wants something more comfortable. And so, in the 1920s,
zh: 贝西想要更舒适的居所。于是，在20世纪20年代，

text: construction begins on what comes to be called Scotty's Castle.
zh: 这座后来被称为“斯科蒂城堡”的建筑开始动工兴建。

text: Today you can tour the opulently furnished rooms, the great hall,
zh: 如今，你可以参观其中陈设奢华的房间、宏伟的大厅，

text: and the music room with its theater organ of 1 ,121 pipes.
zh: 以及配有1121根音管剧院管风琴的音乐室。

text: The Johnsons allow their mansion to be called Scotty's Castle.
zh: 约翰逊夫妇允许人们将这座宅邸称为“斯科蒂城堡”。

text: The three of them become fast friends and Albert Johnson lets it be known that the castle's construction
zh: 三人结为挚友，阿尔伯特·约翰逊公开表示，城堡的建造资金

text: was financed with profits from the gold mine, which never did exist.
zh: 来自那座根本不存在的金矿的“收益”。

text: Though touring Scotty's Castle is a stellar attraction, Death Valley offers sundry other activities
zh: 尽管参观斯科蒂城堡是一大亮点，死亡谷还提供了许多其他活动，

text: of the kind that tone the body. For the rugged, during the cooler months,
zh: 能够锻炼体魄。对于喜欢挑战的人来说，在较凉爽的月份里，

text: hiking is popular and being the Wild West, horseback riding
zh: 徒步是很受欢迎的活动，作为昔日的西部蛮荒之地，骑马也很流行，

text: one can make Death Valley a golfing vacation with a whole new take on golf
zh: 人们还可以把死亡谷之旅变成一次高尔夫假期，体验全新的打法，

text: the 18 hole par 70 furnace Creek course is the world's lowest elevation golf course
zh: 这座18洞、标准杆70杆的熔炉溪球场，是世界上海拔最低的高尔夫球场，

text: there's a noticeable difference in how the ball response. This course is a challenge.
zh: 球的反应会有明显不同，这座球场极具挑战性。

text: Seasonally the stubbornly dry desert yields to brief torrents of winter rain.
zh: 每逢季节，顽固干燥的沙漠会迎来短暂的冬季暴雨，

text: And the desert explodes
zh: 于是沙漠瞬间焕发生机，

text: in a springtime array of exotic flowers.
zh: 绽放出一片春日里奇异花朵盛开的绚丽景象。

text: This is Death Valley when it is most alive.
zh: 这，就是死亡谷最富生机的时刻。
